title: Revising a Depreciation Estimate
event: Reyes Co. bought equipment for $60,000 (no salvage) and has depreciated it straight-line for 4 years of a 10-year life. At the start of year 5, management revises the TOTAL remaining life to 4 more years. Compute the revised annual depreciation.

computationPaths.narration: Estimate changes are handled PROSPECTIVELY: take the remaining book value and spread it over the remaining life. The past stays put.

memorize.body: Revised depreciation = (book value − remaining salvage) ÷ remaining useful life.
No catch-up entry, no restating prior years — a change in ESTIMATE only touches the future.

questions.prompt: BV $36,000 at the start of year 5, 4 years of revised life remain. Revised annual depreciation = ?

distractors.feedback: That's the ORIGINAL rate. The revision respreads today's book value over the NEW remaining life: 36,000 ÷ 4.
You divided the full 60,000 cost by 4. Only the undepreciated 36,000 remains to allocate.
